oligos.name: FS dT30VN (SMART dT30VN)
oligos.role: Oligo-dT reverse-transcription primer carrying the ISPCR handle; primes first-strand cDNA on the mRNA poly(A) tail
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ C6-linker biotin. AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT = ISPCR/SMART handle (shared with the TSO). 30-nt poly(T) + VN anchor (V = A/C/G). Identical sequence to Smart-seq2 Smart dT30VN.

oligos.name: FS TSO
oligos.role: Template-switching oligonucleotide; anneals to the 3' C-tail of first-strand cDNA and templates addition of the ISPCR handle to the 5' end of the transcript
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ biotin. Terminal GGG are riboguanosines (rGrGrG, folded) — riboguanosine replaces the Smart-seq2/3 LNA-G to reduce strand-invasion artifacts. Shares the ISPCR handle with FS dT30VN.

oligos.name: TN5MErev
oligos.role: Mosaic-end reverse strand common to both loaded Tn5 adapters (blocked bottom strand of the transposon end)
oligos.notes: 5' /5Phos/ phosphate. Anneals to the ME of both TN5ME-A and TN5ME-B to form the transposome.

oligos.name: TN5ME-A
oligos.role: i5-side Tn5 transposon adapter (Nextera Read-1 / ME-A top strand)
oligos.notes: Loaded onto Tn5 by annealing with TN5MErev. AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = 19-nt mosaic end.

oligos.name: TN5ME-B
oligos.role: i7-side Tn5 transposon adapter (Nextera Read-2 / ME-B top strand)
oligos.notes: Loaded onto Tn5 by annealing with TN5MErev. AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = 19-nt mosaic end.

oligos.name: Tn5 transposome A (ME-A / MErev)
oligos.role: Assembled loaded transposon end that appends the i5-side adapter during tagmentation
oligos.notes: In-house Tn5 protocol. The 19-bp ME duplex is bound by Tn5; only ME-A is covalently transferred to the 5' of the tagmented fragment.

oligos.name: Tn5 transposome B (ME-B / MErev)
oligos.role: Assembled loaded transposon end that appends the i7-side adapter during tagmentation
oligos.notes: In-house Tn5 protocol. Alternatively the Nextera XT ATM (Illumina) can be used.

oligos.name: Nextera N7xx (i7) index adapter
oligos.role: Enrichment/indexing PCR primer that appends the i7 sample index and P7 flow-cell adapter (binds the ME-B/GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG handle)
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ biotin, phosphorothioate bond between last two nt. CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT = P7. 24 Nextera-XT v2 N7xx (N714–N729) + 48 additional 'extra_i7' indices provided; e.g. N714 index = TCATGAGC, extra_i7_1 = GCCTATCA. Ordered at 5 µM working combos with i5.

oligos.name: Nextera S5xx (i5) index adapter
oligos.role: Enrichment/indexing PCR primer that appends the i5 sample index and P5 flow-cell adapter (binds the ME-A/TCGTCGGCAGCGTC handle)
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ biotin, phosphorothioate bond between last two nt. AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC = P5. 16 Nextera-XT v2 S5xx (S502–S522) + 32 additional 'extra_i5' indices provided; e.g. S502 index = CTCTCTAT, extra_i5_1 = CGACCATT.

oligos.name: STRT-P1-T31 (STRT-dT)
oligos.role: FS-UMI oligo-dT RT primer carrying a partial-P5 handle (Hochgerner et al. STRT-seq-2i); does NOT end in VN
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ biotin. AATGATACGGCGACCACCGA = P5 handle; TCGT linker + 31-nt poly(T). Used in the FS-UMI protocol instead of FS dT30VN.

oligos.name: TSO-UMI (TSO_CTAAC)
oligos.role: FS-UMI template-switching oligo carrying an 8-nt UMI and a 5-nt spacer upstream of the riboguanosines
oligos.notes: 5' /5Biosg/ biotin; terminal GGG are riboguanosines. NNNNNNNN = 8-nt UMI; CTAAC = 5-nt spacer that suppresses strand invasion (recommended variant). Base FS-TSO-UMI omits the spacer (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[UMI:8]GGG); other tested spacers: CAGCA/ATAAC/ATGAC/CTGAC/AAGCA/CATCA/CGTAC.

oligos.name: Tn5-ISPCR forward primer
oligos.role: FS-UMI RT-PCR forward primer; ME + ISPCR fusion that amplifies the internal (TSO-handle) end of the cDNA
oligos.notes: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (ME-A) fused to AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT (ISPCR handle). Added at 0.5 µM in the FS-UMI RT-PCR mix.

oligos.name: DI-PCR-P1A reverse primer
oligos.role: FS-UMI RT-PCR reverse primer that amplifies the oligo-dT (STRT/P5) end of the cDNA
oligos.notes: P5 partial sequence (Hochgerner et al.). Added at 0.1 µM in the FS-UMI RT-PCR mix.

final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC | [SAMPLE_INDEX:8](i5) | TCGTCGGCAGCGTC(ME-A handle / Read1 primer) | AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG(mosaic end) | [CDNA] | CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT(rev mosaic end) | CCGAGCCCACGAGAC(ME-B handle, rev-comp) | [SAMPLE_INDEX:8](i7 rev-comp) | ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTC                 — i5-side Tn5 adapter / Read 1 primer landing site
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT            — reverse mosaic end, 3' of insert
CCGAGCCCACGAGAC                — i7-side Tn5 adapter (ME-B handle, reverse complement)

library_generation.title: Cell lysis & oligo-dT priming
library_generation.summary: A single sorted cell is lysed and its poly(A) mRNA is captured by the biotinylated FS dT30VN oligo-dT after a 72 °C denaturation.
library_generation.note: Cells are FACS-sorted into 384-well plates with 1 µL lysis mix (Triton X-100, dNTPs, FS dT30VN, RNase inhibitor, DTT, FS TSO, dCTP, betaine); 3 min at 72 °C resolves RNA secondary structure and anneals the oligo-dT to the poly(A) tail.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription + template switching
library_generation.summary: SuperScript IV extends the oligo-dT into first-strand cDNA, adds a C-tail at the 5' cap, and switches to the FS TSO, appending the ISPCR handle to both cDNA ends.
library_generation.note: Excess dCTP boosts non-templated C-tailing; the rGrGrG of FS TSO base-pairs with the C-tail and RT copies the TSO. Both cDNA ends now carry the identical AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT handle.
library_generation.product:
5'-[Bio]AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC(T)30VN----cDNA----GGG-3'
                                                    ^ templated off FS TSO handle
   first-strand cDNA flanked by the shared ISPCR handle at both ends

library_generation.title: Combined RT-PCR pre-amplification
library_generation.summary: Full-length cDNA is amplified by semi-suppressive PCR in the same tube using the shared ISPCR handle as a single primer (KAPA HiFi).
library_generation.note: Because FS dT30VN and FS TSO share the 23-nt handle, one handle self-primes both ends. 10–21 cycles depending on cell type/variant (e.g. 19 for HEK293T, 21 for hPBMC).
library_generation.product:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC GGG [==== cDNA ====] (A)n GTACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'
3'- TTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCATG CCC [==== cDNA ====] (T)n CATGAGACGCAACTATGGTGACGAA -5'
            <-------- ISPCR handle both ends -------->

library_generation.title: cDNA cleanup & normalization (skipped in FS-LA)
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is purified with 0.8x SPRI/Sera-Mag beads, QC'd, and diluted to ~100–200 pg/µL.
library_generation.note: Standard FS and FS-UMI perform bead cleanup (0.6x in FS-UMI) plus Bioanalyzer/PicoGreen QC. FS-LA omits cleanup entirely and uses 1 µL of 1:10-diluted raw cDNA directly.
library_generation.product:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT...[full-length cDNA]...ACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'
     (purified, normalized double-stranded cDNA, ~1.8-2.2 kb)

library_generation.title: Tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Tn5 loaded with ME-A/ME-B adapters simultaneously fragments the cDNA and inserts mosaic-end adapters at both ends of each fragment.
library_generation.note: In-house Tn5 (TAPS-Mg/DMF) or Nextera XT ATM. Fragments receive a 9-bp gap and the transferred ME strand; SDS then strips Tn5 from the DNA.
library_generation.product:
5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[==cDNA insert==]                   
                 TCTAC        3'-gap                   CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT...B-5'
   ME-A adapter -----[ insert ]----- ME-B adapter (both ends tagmented)

library_generation.title: Gap-fill & enrichment (indexing) PCR
library_generation.summary: 72 °C gap-fill then PCR with N7xx (i7) + S5xx (i5) primers adds the sample indices and P5/P7 flow-cell adapters.
library_generation.note: 14–24 enrichment cycles depending on pre-amplification level. i5 primer binds the ME-A handle, i7 primer binds the ME-B handle.
library_generation.product:
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
   P5 -- i5 -- ME-A/Read1 -- insert -- rev-ME/ME-B -- i7 -- P7

library_generation.title: Pool & final cleanup
library_generation.summary: Indexed libraries are pooled and purified with 0.8x SPRI beads, then quantified for sequencing.
library_generation.note: Final library ~700–1000 bp; sequenced on any Illumina instrument (NextSeq 550 in the paper).

library_sequencing.note: Nextera Read-1 primer (i5-side ME-A) sequences straight into the tagmented full-length cDNA. Standard FS/FS-LA read mode is 75-8-8-0 and single-end 75 bp is generally sufficient. FS-UMI switches to paired-end 100-8-8-50, in which R1 first reads the UMI block (see the FS-UMI Read 1 entry).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (Nextera Read 1 / ME-A)
   5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG----------->
5'-...TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[==== full-length cDNA ====]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT...-3'
3'-...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[==== full-length cDNA ====]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA...-5'

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt i7 sample index (N7xx). The Index-1 primer anneals to the ME-B handle on the P7 (bottom) strand and reads the index toward P7. Region drawn flipped so the P7 strand is on top (5'->3').
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 (i7) — read off the P7 strand (drawn 5'->3' on top)
5'-...CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT[NNNNNNNN]GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG...-3'
3'-...GTTCGTCTTCTGCCGTATGCTCTA[NNNNNNNN]CAGAGCACCCGAGCCTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC...-5'
              i7 index  <----------[NNNNNNNN]
                        i7 primer: 3'-CAGAGCACCCGAGCCTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC-5'

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt i5 sample index (S5xx). On the NextSeq 550 the i5 index is read from the P5 (top) strand: the primer lands on the TCGTCGGCAGCGTC (i5-side) handle and reads the index toward P5.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 2 (i5) — read off the P5 strand (NextSeq 550)
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[NNNNNNNN]TCGTCGGCAGCGTC...-3'
3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[NNNNNNNN]AGCAGCCGTCGCAG...-5'
            i5 index        <--------[NNNNNNNN]
                              i5 primer: 3'-AGCAGCCGTCGCAG-5'

library_sequencing.note: FS-UMI variant only. R1 reads through the ISPCR handle, then the 8-nt UMI, the CTAAC spacer and the GGG (riboguanosine) block contributed by the TSO-UMI, before entering the 5' end of the cDNA. Read mode 100-8-8-50 (90-8-8-60 / 80-8-8-70 also work). UMIs occasionally land in R2 owing to upstream tagmentation, so umi_tools extracts from both mates.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (Nextera Read 1 / ME-A)
   5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG--------------->
5'-...TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT[NNNNNNNN]CTAACGGG[== cDNA ==]-3'
3'-...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTCTTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCA[NNNNNNNN]GATTGCCC[== cDNA ==]-5'
                                     |----ISPCR handle-----| |--UMI--| |spacer|

title: FLASH-seq
description: FLASH-seq (FS) is a fast, high-sensitivity full-length single-cell RNA-seq method derived from Smart-seq2/3. A single cell is lysed and its polyadenylated mRNA is reverse-transcribed by SuperScript IV using a biotinylated oligo-dT (FS dT30VN); template switching onto a riboguanosine-tailed TSO (FS-TSO) appends a shared ISPCR handle to both cDNA ends, so a single combined RT-PCR pre-amplifies full-length cDNA. The cDNA is then tagmented with Tn5 and enriched with Nextera i5/i7 index primers to build the sequencing library. Reads give full-length gene-body coverage (R1 captures the cDNA insert; i7/i5 index reads identify the cell), enabling isoform and SNP analysis and, in the FS-UMI variant, UMI-based molecule counting. Variants: FS (standard), FS-LA (low-amplification, no cleanup), and FS-UMI (UMI-containing TSO).

original_publication.title: Fast and highly sensitive full-length single-cell RNA sequencing using FLASH-seq

authors.name: Vincent Hahaut

authors.affiliation: Institute of Molecular and Clinical Ophthalmology Basel (IOB); Department of Ophthalmology, University of Basel, Basel, Switzerland

authors.affiliation: Chemical Biology and Therapeutics, Novartis Institutes for Biomedical Research, Basel, Switzerland

throughput.summary: Plate-based full-length scRNA-seq (384-well); complete FS protocol in ~4.5 h with <1 h hands-on for FS-LA. Detects ~12,000–13,000 genes per HEK293T cell; cost <$1/cell.
throughput.cells: Hundreds to ~1,536 cells per experiment (384 cells/plate; multiple plates in parallel)
throughput.rna: Full-length transcript coverage; ~8x more cDNA yield than SS2/SS3 at equal cycles; ~1.8–2.2 kb average cDNA

publication.statistical_model: UMI-based molecule counting via umi_tools deduplication (FS-UMI variant); differential-expression cell-type markers called with MAST (hurdle model); group comparisons by two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum and Dunn's tests, cell-to-cell similarity by Kendall's tau.

other.label: Enzymes/additives
other.value: SuperScript IV RT + KAPA HiFi in a single RT-PCR mix; excess dCTP (3.3 mM) to boost C-tailing/template switching; betaine

other.label: Applications
other.value: Isoform detection, allele/SNP calling at single-cell level, TCR reconstruction; validated on HEK293T, hPBMCs and week-18 retinal organoids

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed with bcl2fastq, optionally adapter/primer-trimmed with BBDuk, aligned as full-length transcripts to the genome with STAR, filtered and sorted with samtools, and assigned to genes with featureCounts to build a per-cell read-count matrix. The FS-UMI variant additionally extracts the 8-nt UMI (bounded by the ISPCR handle and the CTAAC-GGG spacer) with umi_tools, splits the 5' UMI reads from the internal full-length reads, maps them separately, and deduplicates molecules with umi_tools count. FS-UMI reconciles the fact that UMIs can appear in R1 or (rarely) R2 due to upstream tagmentation. Post-processing follows Smart-seq2 guidelines (Seurat/scanpy/OSCA).
data_processing.statistical_model: Count-based quantification with no distributional model; the FS-UMI variant applies umi_tools directional UMI deduplication for molecule counting.
